answer:
## Declaring the two flags

### Root-level `--verbose` (seen by every descendant)

Put it on the root command's **`PersistentFlags()`**. Persistent flags declared *on* a command are inherited by that command's descendants ŌĆö that's exactly the ADR-02 example of "`--verbose` on root, honored by every subcommand":

```go
var verbose bool

rootCmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "myapp"}
rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&verbose, "verbose", "v", false, "verbose output")
```

`BoolVarP` is pflag's POSIX-style call that creates both `--verbose` and `-v` in one shot (pflag is imported as `flag "github.com/spf13/pflag"` throughout cobra).

### Child-only `--dry-run` (child and its descendants)

If only one command and its descendants should see `--dry-run`, put it on **that child's** flag set:

- Use `childCmd.Flags()` if you want it only on the child itself (local/non-inherited).
- Use `childCmd.PersistentFlags()` if you want it visible to the child *and* the child's own subcommands.

```go
var dryRun bool

// Only this command:
childCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&dryRun, "dry-run", false, "plan without executing")

// This command AND its subcommands:
childCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&dryRun, "dry-run", false, "plan without executing")
```

Either way, siblings and the root never see it, because a persistent flag only descends; it does not ascend to the parent or spread to siblings.

---

## `Flags()` vs `PersistentFlags()` vs `LocalFlags()` vs `InheritedFlags()`

Per ADR-02, each `*cobra.Command` actually owns several distinct pflag `FlagSet`s (see `command.go:155-166`), and the four accessors expose different views of them:

| Method | Backed by | What it contains |
|---|---|---|
| `PersistentFlags()` | `pflags` | Flags **declared on this command** that will be inherited by descendants. This is where you *register* inheritable flags. |
| `Flags()` | `flags` | The **full resolved set** visible to this command at execute time ŌĆö local flags declared here, plus any persistent flags merged in from this command and all ancestors. This is what pflag actually parses against. |
| `LocalFlags()` | `lflags` cache | Only flags **declared on this command** (both its local and its own persistent) ŌĆö excludes anything inherited from ancestors. |
| `InheritedFlags()` | `iflags` cache | Only flags **from ancestor `PersistentFlags()`** ŌĆö excludes flags declared on this command itself. |

Conceptually: `Flags() Ōēł LocalFlags() Ōł¬ InheritedFlags()`.

### How the merge actually happens

The "every descendant sees root `--verbose`" behavior is not magic ŌĆö it's `mergePersistentFlags` (`command.go:1900-1901`) calling pflag's `AddFlagSet` to fold ancestor persistent flag sets into the child's `Flags()` at execute time:

```go
c.Flags().AddFlagSet(c.PersistentFlags())
c.Flags().AddFlagSet(c.parentsPflags)
```

### A gotcha worth knowing

From ADR-02's consequences: `PersistentFlags()` creates its FlagSet **lazily** and it is **not shared** with `Flags()` until the next merge, which happens inside `execute()`. If you register `--verbose` on `rootCmd.PersistentFlags()` and then, before calling `rootCmd.Execute()`, try to `rootCmd.Flags().Lookup("verbose")`, it will return nil and fail silently. Either look it up via `PersistentFlags().Lookup(...)`, or do the lookup from inside a command's `Run`/`RunE` where parsing has already happened.

### Reading them in a subcommand's Run

Because `--verbose` is merged in before `Run` fires, the simplest pattern is just to close over the bound variable:

```go
childCmd.RunE = func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    if verbose { /* ... */ }       // bound via BoolVarP on root
    if dryRun  { /* ... */ }       // bound on child
    return nil
}
```

If you don't want to bind to a variable, `cmd.Flags().GetBool("verbose")` works from any descendant at run time, because by then `Flags()` has the merged view.

1	---
2	id: ADR-02
3	title: Flag parsing uses spf13/pflag, not the Go standard library flag package
4	status: accepted
5	severity: hard
6	symbols:
7	  - pflag
8	  - flag.FlagSet
9	  - Flags
10	  - PersistentFlags
11	  - LocalFlags
12	  - InheritedFlags
13	  - ShorthandLookup
14	  - AddFlagSet
15	  - SetAnnotation
16	  - ContinueOnError
17	  - FParseErrWhitelist
18	  - mergePersistentFlags
19	---
20	
21	# ADR-02: Flag parsing uses spf13/pflag, not the Go standard library flag package
22	
23	## Context
24	
25	Go's standard library ships a `flag` package. It parses command-line flags.
26	The vast majority of small Go programs use it. The Go team maintains it.
27	The obvious default choice when designing a CLI framework is to build
28	on top of it.
29	
30	Cobra does not. Cobra's only non-stdlib runtime dependency is
31	`github.com/spf13/pflag` (`go.mod`), imported in every non-trivial file
32	as `flag "github.com/spf13/pflag"` (see `cobra.go:30`, `flag_groups.go:22`,
33	`command.go` import block). The stdlib `flag` package is imported only for
34	the narrow purpose of interop (`command.go` uses stdlib `flag.CommandLine`
35	at line 1918 to adopt stdlib flags).
36	
37	The stdlib `flag` package has several properties that make it unsuitable
38	as the substrate for a "modern CLI" framework in the `git`/`kubectl` mold
39	that cobra is explicitly targeting:
40	
41	- **No POSIX long/short flags.** Stdlib `flag` treats `-v` and `--v`
42	  identically; there is no concept of a one-character short form paired
43	  with a multi-character long form (`-v` / `--verbose`). POSIX-style CLIs
44	  expect both.
45	- **No flag annotations.** Stdlib `flag.Flag` is a closed struct; user
46	  code cannot attach metadata to a flag. Cobra needs to mark flags as
47	  required, as filename-completable, as participating in a mutually
48	  exclusive group ŌĆö all of which require an extensible metadata channel
49	  on the flag.
50	- **No FlagSet-to-FlagSet merge.** Stdlib `flag.FlagSet` has no way to
51	  copy or import another FlagSet's flags. Cobra's persistent-flag
52	  inheritance, where a parent's `--config` propagates to every
53	  descendant, requires merging a parent's FlagSet into each child's
54	  FlagSet at execute time.
55	- **`flag.ExitOnError` on first parse failure.** Stdlib `flag` has
56	  three error modes; cobra needs to keep parsing past flag errors to
57	  produce coherent help and suggestion output, and to let the calling
58	  code decide whether to exit.
59	- **No shorthand lookup.** Stdlib has no API to find a flag by its
60	  single-character short form. Cobra needs this to detect collisions
61	  when auto-adding `-h` and `-v`.
62	
63	The three options available when cobra was designed were: extend stdlib
64	(impossible ŌĆö it's closed), fork stdlib and extend the fork (pflag
65	existed as a spf13 fork), or write a completely new flag parser from
66	scratch. Cobra chose the fork.
67	
68	## Decision
69	
70	Cobra depends on `github.com/spf13/pflag` exclusively for flag parsing.
71	The dependency is declared in `go.mod` and the type is aliased as
72	`flag` in every file that touches flags:
73	
74	```go
75	import flag "github.com/spf13/pflag"
76	```
77	
78	(`cobra.go:30`, `flag_groups.go:22`, and command.go's import block)
79	
80	The `Command` struct owns four pflag FlagSets (`command.go:155-166`):
81	- `flags *flag.FlagSet` ŌĆö the full, resolved set visible to this command
82	- `pflags *flag.FlagSet` ŌĆö persistent flags declared *on* this command,
83	  inherited by descendants
84	- `lflags *flag.FlagSet` ŌĆö local-flags cache (only flags declared on
85	  this command, not inherited)
86	- `iflags *flag.FlagSet` ŌĆö inherited-flags cache (only flags from
87	  ancestors, not declared here)
88	- `parentsPflags *flag.FlagSet` ŌĆö all ancestor persistent flags,
89	  used during the merge
90	
91	Cobra uses pflag's POSIX-compliant short+long API pervasively. The
92	default version flag is added via `BoolP("version", "v", false, usage)`
93	(`command.go:1229`), which creates `-v` and `--version` simultaneously
94	ŌĆö a single stdlib call cannot express this.
95	
96	Cobra uses pflag's annotation map as a typed extension point. Three
97	annotations defined in cobra itself:
98	
99	```go
100	FlagSetByCobraAnnotation     = "cobra_annotation_flag_set_by_cobra"
101	CommandDisplayNameAnnotation = "cobra_annotation_command_display_name"
102	```
103	(`cobra.go:34-35`)
104	
105	Three more defined for flag groups:
106	
107	```go
108	requiredAsGroupAnnotation   = "cobra_annotation_required_if_others_set"
109	oneRequiredAnnotation       = "cobra_annotation_one_required"
110	mutuallyExclusiveAnnotation = "cobra_annotation_mutually_exclusive"
111	```
112	(`flag_groups.go:26-28`)
113	
114	Additional annotations for completion:
115	- `BashCompFilenameExt`, `BashCompCustom`, `BashCompOneRequiredFlag`,
116	  `BashCompSubdirsInDir` (`bash_completions.go:29-34`)
117	
118	None of these are possible on stdlib `flag.Flag`.
119	
120	Persistent-flag inheritance is impl
